channel.title: Context Engineering Daily
channel.description: Daily news and research updates in AI Context Engineering, Prompt Engineering, RAG, and LLM development

item.title: Evaluating Prompting Strategies for Chart Question Answering with Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2603.22288v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Prompting strategies affect LLM reasoning performance, but their role in chart-based QA remains underexplored. We present a systematic evaluation of four widely used prompting paradigms (Zero-Shot, Few-Shot, Zero-Shot Chain-of-Thought, and Few-Shot Ch...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> prompt, reasoning, LLM, framework, GPT | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: MERIT: Memory-Enhanced Retrieval for Interpretable Knowledge Tracing
item.description: arXiv:2603.22289v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Knowledge Tracing (KT) models students' evolving knowledge states to predict future performance, serving as a foundation for personalized education. While traditional deep learning models achieve high accuracy, they often lack interpretability. Large ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> retrieval, context, CoT, reasoning, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Less is More: Adapting Text Embeddings for Low-Resource Languages with Small Scale Noisy Synthetic Data
item.description: arXiv:2603.22290v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Low-resource languages (LRLs) often lack high-quality, large-scale datasets for training effective text embedding models, hindering their application in tasks like retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) and semantic search. In this work, we challenge th...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> retrieval, alignment, LLM, RAG, example | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Evaluating Large Language Models' Responses to Sexual and Reproductive Health Queries in Nepali
item.description: arXiv:2603.22291v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As Large Language Models (LLMs) become integrated into daily life, they are increasingly used for personal queries, including Sexual and Reproductive Health (SRH), allowing users to chat anonymously without fear of judgment. However, current evaluatio...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, LLM, GPT, product, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: TIPS: Turn-Level Information-Potential Reward Shaping for Search-Augmented LLMs
item.description: arXiv:2603.22293v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Search-augmented large language models (LLMs) trained with reinforcement learning (RL) have achieved strong results on open-domain question answering (QA), but training still remains a significant challenge. The optimization is often unstable due to s...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> API, reasoning, LLM, RAG, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Whether, Not Which: Mechanistic Interpretability Reveals Dissociable Affect Reception and Emotion Categorization in LLMs
item.description: arXiv:2603.22295v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models appear to develop internal representations of emotion -- "emotion circuits," "emotion neurons," and structured emotional manifolds have been reported across multiple model families. But every study making these claims uses stimul...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> alignment, LLM, study, release, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Sparse but Critical: A Token-Level Analysis of Distributional Shifts in RLVR Fine-Tuning of LLMs
item.description: arXiv:2603.22446v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) has significantly improved reasoning in large language models (LLMs), yet the token-level mechanisms underlying these improvements remain unclear. We present a systematic empirical study of RLVR's ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, LLM, fine-tuning, study, analysis | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Towards Automated Community Notes Generation with Large Vision Language Models for Combating Contextual Deception
item.description: arXiv:2603.22453v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Community Notes have emerged as an effective crowd-sourced mechanism for combating online deception on social media platforms. However, its reliance on human contributors limits both the timeliness and scalability. In this work, we study the automated...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> retrieval, context, research, framework, GPT | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: LLM-guided headline rewriting for clickability enhancement without clickbait
item.description: arXiv:2603.22459v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Enhancing reader engagement while preserving informational fidelity is a central challenge in controllable text generation for news media. Optimizing news headlines for reader engagement is often conflated with clickbait, resulting in exaggerated or m...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, LLM, study, model, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Functional Component Ablation Reveals Specialization Patterns in Hybrid Language Model Architectures
item.description: arXiv:2603.22473v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Hybrid language models combining attention with state space models (SSMs) or linear attention offer improved efficiency, but whether both components are genuinely utilized remains unclear. We present a functional component ablation framework applied t...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, compression, analysis, model, attention | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Memory Bear AI Memory Science Engine for Multimodal Affective Intelligence: A Technical Report
item.description: arXiv:2603.22306v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Affective judgment in real interaction is rarely a purely local prediction problem. Emotional meaning often depends on prior trajectory, accumulated context, and multimodal evidence that may be weak, noisy, or incomplete at the current moment. Althoug...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> retrieval, context, framework, memory, multimodal | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Dynamic Fusion-Aware Graph Convolutional Neural Network for Multimodal Emotion Recognition in Conversations
item.description: arXiv:2603.22345v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multimodal emotion recognition in conversations (MERC) aims to identify and understand the emotions expressed by speakers during utterance interaction from multiple modalities (e.g., text, audio, images, etc.). Existing studies have shown that GCN can...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> prompt, RAG, audio, multimodal, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Session Risk Memory (SRM): Temporal Authorization for Deterministic Pre-Execution Safety Gates
item.description: arXiv:2603.22350v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Deterministic pre-execution safety gates evaluate whether individual agent actions are compatible with their assigned roles. While effective at per-action authorization, these systems are structurally blind to distributed attacks that decompose harmfu...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> memory, RAG, framework, model, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: From Static Templates to Dynamic Runtime Graphs: A Survey of Workflow Optimization for LLM Agents
item.description: arXiv:2603.22386v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language model (LLM)-based systems are becoming increasingly popular for solving tasks by constructing executable workflows that interleave LLM calls, information retrieval, tool use, code execution, memory updates, and verification. This survey...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> retrieval, memory, LLM, framework, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Computational Arbitrage in AI Model Markets
item.description: arXiv:2603.22404v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Consider a market of competing model providers selling query access to models with varying costs and capabilities. Customers submit problem instances and are willing to pay up to a budget for a verifiable solution. An arbitrageur efficiently allocates...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, GPT, study, model, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Efficient Embedding-based Synthetic Data Generation for Complex Reasoning Tasks
item.description: arXiv:2603.22294v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Synthetic Data Generation (SDG), leveraging Large Language Models (LLMs), has recently been recognized and broadly adopted as an effective approach to improve the performance of smaller but more resource and compute efficient LLMs through fine-tuning....<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, LLM, RAG, example, fine-tuning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Between the Layers Lies the Truth: Uncertainty Estimation in LLMs Using Intra-Layer Local Information Scores
item.description: arXiv:2603.22299v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are often confidently wrong, making reliable uncertainty estimation (UE) essential. Output-based heuristics are cheap but brittle, while probing internal representations is effective yet high-dimensional and hard to transf...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, RAG, model, arxiv, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Scaling Attention via Feature Sparsity
item.description: arXiv:2603.22300v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Scaling Transformers to ultra-long contexts is bottlenecked by the $O(n^2 d)$ cost of self-attention. Existing methods reduce this cost along the sequence axis through local windows, kernel approximations, or token-level sparsity, but these approaches...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> retrieval, context, GPT, embedding, attention | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Latent Semantic Manifolds in Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2603.22301v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) perform internal computations in continuous vector spaces yet produce discrete tokens -- a fundamental mismatch whose geometric consequences remain poorly understood. We develop a mathematical framework that interprets LLM...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, framework, LLM, compression, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Sample Transform Cost-Based Training-Free Hallucination Detector for Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2603.22303v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Hallucinations in large language models (LLMs) remain a central obstacle to trustworthy deployment, motivating detectors that are accurate, lightweight, and broadly applicable. Since an LLM with a prompt defines a conditional distribution, we argue th...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> prompt, framework, LLM, RAG, embedding | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Mitigating Premature Discretization with Progressive Quantization for Robust Vector Tokenization
item.description: arXiv:2603.22304v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Vector Quantization (VQ) has become the cornerstone of tokenization for many multimodal Large Language Models and diffusion synthesis. However, existing VQ paradigms suffer from a fundamental conflict: they enforce discretization before the encoder ha...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> multimodal, model, image, experiment, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: CN-Buzz2Portfolio: A Chinese-Market Dataset and Benchmark for LLM-Based Macro and Sector Asset Allocation from Daily Trending Financial News
item.description: arXiv:2603.22305v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are rapidly transitioning from static Natural Language Processing (NLP) tasks including sentiment analysis and event extraction to acting as dynamic decision-making agents in complex financial environments. However, the ev...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> API, alignment, reasoning, LLM, compression | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Context-Engineering - "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.
item.description: "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, prompt, context window, prompt engineering | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: awesome-prompt-engineering - A curated list of resources, tools, papers, and platforms for prompt engineering in large language models (LLMs) and generative AI.
item.description: A curated list of resources, tools, papers, and platforms for prompt engineering in large language models (LLMs) and generative AI.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> prompt, LLM, prompt engineering, model, platform | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: ThinkSound - [NeurIPS 2025] PyTorch implementation of [ThinkSound], a unified framework for generating audio from any modality, guided by Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning.

item.title: LightRAG - [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"
item.description: [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> retrieval, RAG, augmented | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: KAG - KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.
item.description: KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> retrieval, reasoning, LLM, RAG, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Kiln - Build, Evaluate, and Optimize AI Systems. Includes evals, RAG, agents, fine-tuning, synthetic data generation, dataset management, MCP, and more.
item.description: Build, Evaluate, and Optimize AI Systems. Includes evals, RAG, agents, fine-tuning, synthetic data generation, dataset management, MCP, and more.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> fine-tuning, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>

item.title: Intelligence Inertia: Physical Principles and Applications
item.description: arXiv:2603.22347v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: While Landauer's principle establishes the fundamental thermodynamic floor for information erasure and Fisher Information provides a metric for local curvature in parameter space, these classical frameworks function effectively only as approximations ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, analysis, model, experiment, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: STEM Agent: A Self-Adapting, Tool-Enabled, Extensible Architecture for Multi-Protocol AI Agent Systems
item.description: arXiv:2603.22359v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Current AI agent frameworks commit early to a single interaction protocol, a fixed tool integration strategy, and static user models, limiting their deployment across diverse interaction paradigms. To address these constraints, we introduce STEM Agent...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, memory, framework, model, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: cosmos-reason1 - Cosmos-Reason1 models understand the physical common sense and generate appropriate embodied decisions in natural language through long chain-of-thought reasoning processes.

item.title: The Efficiency Attenuation Phenomenon: A Computational Challenge to the Language of Thought Hypothesis
item.description: arXiv:2603.22312v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This paper computationally investigates whether thought requires a language-like format, as posited by the Language of Thought (LoT) hypothesis. We introduce the ``AI Private Language'' thought experiment: if two artificial agents develop an efficient...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, paper, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: AI Mental Models: Learned Intuition and Deliberation in a Bounded Neural Architecture
item.description: arXiv:2603.22561v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This paper asks whether a bounded neural architecture can exhibit a meaningful division of labor between intuition and deliberation on a classic 64-item syllogistic reasoning benchmark. More broadly, the benchmark is relevant to ongoing debates about ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, example, model, experiment, vision | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Beyond Hard Constraints: Budget-Conditioned Reachability For Safe Offline Reinforcement Learning
item.description: arXiv:2603.22292v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Sequential decision making using Markov Decision Process underpins many realworld applications. Both model-based and model free methods have achieved strong results in these settings. However, real-world tasks must balance reward maximization with saf...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> analysis, arxiv, model, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.description: arXiv:2603.22302v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: With the development of information technology, the application of artificial intelligence and machine learning in the field of education shows great potential. This study aims to explore how to utilize K-means clustering algorithm to provide accurate...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> study, arxiv, research | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Full waveform inversion method based on diffusion model
item.description: arXiv:2603.22307v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Seismic full-waveform inversion is a core technology for obtaining high-resolution subsurface model parameters. However, its highly nonlinear characteristics and strong dependence on the initial model often lead to the inversion process getting trappe...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, paper, model, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: OpenViking - OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.
item.description: OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> memory, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Claude-Code-Everything-You-Need-to-Know - The ultimate all-in-one guide to mastering Claude Code. From setup, prompt engineering, commands, hooks, workflows, automation, and integrations, to MCP servers, tools, and the BMAD method—packed with step-by-step tutorials, real-world examples, and expert strategies to make this the global go-to repo for Claude mastery.
item.description: The ultimate all-in-one guide to mastering Claude Code. From setup, prompt engineering, commands, hooks, workflows, automation, and integrations, to MCP servers, tools, and the BMAD method—packed with step-by-step tutorials, real-world examples, and expert strategies to make this the global go-to repo for Claude mastery.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> prompt, step-by-step, prompt engineering, example, tool | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
